sections.paragraph:
(“pining,” see the root דּוּב ), [Dibon], pr.n.-

(1) Of a town on the borders of Moab, on the northern shore of Arnon, built, i.e. restored by the Gadites ( Num 32:34), whence called Dibon-Gad ( Num 33:45), afterwards granted to the Reubenites ( Jos 13:9, 17 Jos 13:17), afterwards again occupied by the Moabites ( Isa 15:2 Jerem. 48:18 , 22 Jer 48:22). It is now called Dhîbân, see Burckhardt’s Travels, ii. p. 633. Once ( Isa 15:9) by a change of the letters מ and ב it is written דִּימֹון, so as to form a paronomasia with the word דָּם.

(2) Of a town in the tribe of Judah, Neh 11:25, called דִּימֹונָה Jos 15:22.
